reasoning: The Federal Reserve has just implemented a rate cut on October 29/30, 2025, bringing the Prime Rate down from 7.25% to 7.00%. The Prime Rate is directly tied to the Federal Funds Rate (it is exactly 3% higher). Because the Fed is currently in an active cutting cycle (having also cut rates in September 2025), the probability that they will reverse course and INCREASE the rate from its current 7.00% level over the next few months is extremely low. There is no scheduled FOMC meeting before the first resolution date (Nov 16, 2025), making a hike a near-zero probability. For the second date (Dec 9, 2025), even if an early December FOMC meeting occurs, a hike immediately following a cut is historically unprecedented outside of massive exogenous shocks. By the third date (Feb 7, 2026), there is slightly more time for inflation data to surprise to the upside and prompt a hike at a late-January FOMC meeting, but this remains a low-probability tail risk. Thus, probabilities are extremely low and slowly increase with the horizon to reflect small tail distributions.
